title: Implementing a Database-Driven Resource Server with SQL Queries
statement:
Create a RESTful API server using Express.js that interacts with an in-memory SQLite database. The server should handle GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE requests for managing books stored in the database. Each book has a unique ID, title, author, and publication year.

### Requirements

- The server should start listening on port 3000.
- All incoming requests should be parsed as JSON.
- Implement the following endpoints:

#### `GET /api/books`

- Return a list of all existing books in the database.
- The response should be in JSON format and include each book's ID, title, author, and publication year.

#### `POST /api/books`

- Create a new book in the database based on the provided JSON payload.
- The payload should contain the title, author, and publication year of the book.
- The server should generate a unique ID for the new book and return it in the response.
- The response should be in JSON format and include the ID, title, author, and publication year of the newly created book.

#### `GET /api/books/:id`

- Return the book with the specified ID in the database.
- The response should be in JSON format and include the ID, title, author, and publication year of the book.

#### `PUT /api/books/:id`

- Update the book with the specified ID in the database based on the provided JSON payload.
- The payload should contain the title, author, or publication year of the book.
- The server should return the updated book in the response.
- The response should be in JSON format and include the ID, title, author, and publication year of the updated book.

#### `DELETE /api/books/:id`

- Delete the book with the specified ID from the database.
- The server should return a success message in the response.

### Constraints

- Must use the in-memory SQLite database provided.
constraints: Must use the in-memory SQLite database provided

endpoints.description: Return a list of all existing books in the database
Create a new book in the database based on the provided JSON payload
Return the book with the specified ID in the database
Update the book with the specified ID in the database based on the provided JSON payload
Delete the book with the specified ID from the database
